entries.title: Atomic Structure
entries.content: Atoms have a nucleus (protons + neutrons) surrounded by electrons in shells. Atomic number = protons; mass number = protons + neutrons.

entries.title: pH Scale
entries.content: Measures acidity/alkalinity from 0–14. pH 7 is neutral; below 7 acidic, above 7 basic. pH = −log[H⁺].

entries.title: Mole and Avogadro's Number
entries.content: One mole contains 6.022×10²³ particles. Molar mass (g/mol) lets you convert between grams and moles.

entries.title: Chemical Bonding Types
entries.content: Ionic: electron transfer (metal + nonmetal). Covalent: electron sharing. Metallic: electron sea in metals.

entries.title: Balancing Chemical Equations
entries.content: Same number of each atom on both sides. Start with complex molecules, leave H and O for last. Coefficients multiply entire formulas.
